articles.title: Senate Plunges Into Vote Marathon as G.O.P. Advances ICE Funding Boost
articles.summary: Republicans pressed to pass a budget plan with a $70 billion increase for immigration enforcement. But first, in an overnight session, they beat back Democratic proposals aimed at lowering costs.

articles.title: Navy Secretary John Phelan Is Leaving the Pentagon and the Trump Administration
articles.summary: John Phelan is leaving the Pentagon after months of tension with Pete Hegseth and other Pentagon leaders. The tumult comes as the Navy has been engaged in war with Iran.

articles.title: War in Iran Gives New Fuel to a Tax Debate in Australia
articles.summary: The world’s third-largest exporter of natural gas, Australia has been too lenient in taxing lucrative gas exports, many in the country argue.

articles.title: Combative, Defensive and Occasionally Contrite, Kennedy Walks a Fine Line
articles.summary: In four days of congressional testimony, the health secretary sought to please the White House and his MAHA base at the same time.

articles.title: Chemical leak at a W.Va. plant kills 2 people, sends 30 more to hospitals, officials say
articles.summary: The leak occurred at the Catalyst Refiners plant, a silver recovery business. An emergency management official says workers were preparing to shut down at least part of the facility when the leak occu

articles.title: Pentagon says Navy secretary is leaving, the latest departure of a top defense leader
articles.summary: Pentagon spokesman Sean Parnell said John Phelan, the Navy's top civilian official, was "departing the administration, effective immediately." Navy Undersecretary Hung Cao will become acting secretary

articles.title: Tesla's making money. But it's planning to spend an awful lot more
articles.summary: Tesla's profits were up from this time last year. But the company warned investors to prepare for expensive investments in next-generation technology like humanoid robots and AI.

articles.title: How the War Powers Act Could Pressure Trump to End the Iran War
articles.summary: A decades-old law allows the president to wage war without congressional approval for 60 days, then limits his options for continuing. President Trump may seek to get around it.

articles.title: F.B.I. Said to Have Investigated Times Reporter After Article on Patel’s Girlfriend
articles.summary: The bureau said it is not pursuing a case, but the scrutiny is an example of the Trump administration weighing whether to criminalize routine news gathering.

articles.title: RFK Jr. Says His Department Advises All Children to Get Measles Vaccine
articles.summary: Testifying on Capitol Hill, Robert F. Kennedy Jr. continued to back away from his criticism of the measles shot. But he spoke on behalf of his department, not himself.

articles.title: Iran Again Tightens Its Grip on Shipping in the Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Traffic in the strait has all but halted as Iran renews its attacks, striking two vessels on Wednesday. More than 300 ships linked to Iran have passed through the strait since the war began.

articles.title: The tariff refund process has begun for businesses. What about customers?
articles.summary: While shipping companies are pledging refunds for customers who directly paid tariff fees, the situation is much trickier for retailers.

articles.title: Inside Linda McMahon's effort to dismantle the Department of Education
articles.summary: A former pro-wrestling executive, McMahon is now the education secretary Trump tasked with abolishing the agency. New Yorker writer Zach Helfand explains how her WWE experience led her to this role.

articles.title: Iranians are leaving the country just to access the internet
articles.summary: Iran has cut off its access to the global internet. To find an internet connection, some Iranians are traveling across the border with Turkey — even just to make video calls and then go back home.

articles.title: Gene therapy for a rare type of deafness shows lasting results
articles.summary: Researchers say a gene therapy allowed deaf children and adults as old as 32 to hear for the first time. The benefits have persisted for more than two years for some patients.

articles.title: Family influencers make the lifestyle look good. But kids pay the price, new book says
articles.summary: What does it mean to monetize your offspring? To turn their childhood into content? In Like, Follow, Subscribe Fortesa Latifi explores what drives parents to become family influencers.

articles.title: Greetings from an Islamabad park, a peaceful vantage point in an uncertain world
articles.summary: The park, near the venue where inconclusive Iran-U.S. peace talks took place this month, provides respite to those who visit it.

articles.title: Ships attacked in Strait of Hormuz. And, VA voters approve redistricting effort
articles.summary: Two ships came under attack in the Strait of Hormuz, hours after Trump extended the ceasefire with Iran. And, Virginia voters approved a measure allowing Democrats to redraw the congressional map.

articles.title: Iran says it seized ships in Strait of Hormuz as U.S. blockade continues amid ceasefire
articles.summary: Three vessels came under fire in the Strait of Hormuz, after the U.S. and Iran failed to meet in Pakistan for talks to end the war and as President Trump extended the ceasefire but kept the blockade.

articles.title: Priceless 2,500-year-old golden helmet returned to Romania after Dutch museum raid
articles.summary: The ornate Cotofenesti helmet, which was stolen in January 2025 while on loan to a Dutch museum, was recovered last month.

articles.title: Mexico to beef up security at tourist sites after shooting at pyramids
articles.summary: Mexico's government is boosting security at tourist sites in preparation for the World Cup after a man opened fire at the Teotihuacan pyramids.

articles.title: As Pakistan seeks to broker U.S.–Iran peace, citizens watch for gains at home
articles.summary: As Pakistan works to broker peace between the U.S. and Iran, many Pakistanis are watching closely to see whether the diplomatic boost will translate into improvements at home.

articles.title: What would happen next if Iran agreed to surrender its highly enriched uranium
articles.summary: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Scott Roecker of the Nuclear Threat Initiative about how the U.S. might go about moving highly enriched uranium out of Iran.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
